properties.remark: VIDEO ON FB SHOWS FLASH FLOODING AT BEAVER CREEK BRIDGE BETWEEN RIMROCK AND LAKE MONTEZUMA.
LARGE ROCK SLIDE ACROSS STATE ROUTE 89A.
FROM REPORT ON FACEBOOK.
[DELAYED REPORT] TIME ESTIMATED. EXTREMELY HEAVY RAIN AND HAIL LED TO THE FLOODING OF ROSE CREEK OVER HORNET DR.
A COUNTY ALERT STREAM GAGE ON BLACK CANYON CREEK AT MAREN AVE REPORTED A 1.97 FT RISE.
White river near Fort Apache increased 400 cubic feet per second. Observation obtained from CBRFC River gauge (WHTA3).
Corrects previous flash flood report from 8 WSW Canyon Day. White river near Fort Apache increased 400 cubic feet per second. Observation obtained from CBRFC River gauge (WHTA3).
Tree limbs broken and trees uprooted. Reports of shingles blown off and a power outage for much of Page.
Video of brief landspout tornado. Time estimated.
Wunderground station KAZPRESC1037 clocked wind gust of 64 mph.
Black Canyon Creek near Cleator gage increased to 2.92 ft. and 1026 cfs.
Corrects previous flash flood report from 3 ESE Cordes Junction. AFMA3 increased to 4.06 ft. and 71.2 cfs.
AFMA3 increased to 4.06 ft. and 71.2 cfs.
*Corrected* Sedona Airport AWOS measured a 52 knot (60 mph) wind gust at 315 Arizona Time (MST) as thunderstorm outflow moved through.
Sedona Airport AWOS measured a 52 knot (60 mph) wind gust at 315 Arizona Time (MST).
3.84 feet peak following heavy rainfall. Rose from 1.59 feet within a couple hours.
Quarter sized hail reported in Yavapai Hills area.
Hail around an inch reported near Costco.
Video of flash flood in Big Bug Creek at Mayer (from social media).
Automated stream gauge on Havasu Creek measured a 1.5 foot rise.
County official submitted an image of a flooded roadway on the west end of Copeland.
Corrects previous flash flood report from 6 NNW Doney Park. Broadcast Media video of flash flooding at the Wupatki Trails.
Broadcast Media video of flash flooding at the Wupatki Trails.
Estimated 60 mph winds, along with frequently lightning and heavy rainfall.
Automated stream gauge on Cataract Creek measured a 6 foot rise.
Roads in the area were impassable.
Spotter showed an photo of a mixture of dime, nickel and quarter sized hail.
Landspout spotted 10 miles north of Cameron, Arizona at 1:50 MDT. Only visible lofted debris was dust. Dissipated within 10 minutes after sighting. Video on Twitter.
Flash flood through Lower Antelope Canyon. Time of event estimated. Lower Antelope Canyon tours were closed on 8/12/2023 to clean out the mud and debris.
Delayed Report. Strong thunderstorm wind gusts observed in Face Canyon (Lake Powell). Considerable blowing sand.
Indian Route 15 is closed due to flooding at mile post 95 between Cornfields and Greasewood. Report from Navajo Nation Road Conditions Facebook page.

About 1 to 2.5 inches of rain fell in about 2 hours flooding homes, buildings and destroying cars. Highway 64 was closed, shelter in place orders were put into place and children were stuck in their school because they could not safely get home.
Debris reported over Copeland Lane, road grader needed to clear.

The Paria River received ample rainfall input from thunderstorms associated with the remnants of tropical system Harold. Said thunderstorms traveled south along the Paria Basin and a flash flood warning had been issued during the time of rainfall. The Lees Ferry gauge measured a sudden rise in water levels from this rainfall starting at 8:15 PM MST at 4.78 feet and crested at 11 PM that night at 6.64 feet.

Corrects previous flash flood report from 2 SE Arizona Snowbowl. Heavy rain over Agassiz Peak caused a flash flood on the southwest side of the peak. The Kachina Trail was covered with rocks and mud from the flood in two different locations over 300 yards apart. A much smaller flow reached Freidlein Prairie (FR522). Some of the rocks in the flow were 3 to 5 feet in diameter.

Heavy rain over Fremont Peak caused a flash flood on the southwest side of the peak. The Kachina Trail and Freidlein Prairie Road were covered with rocks and mud from the flood. Some of the rocks in the flow were 3 to 5 feet in diameter.

Corrects previous debris flow report from 2 SE Arizona Snowbowl. Heavy rain over Agassiz Peak caused a flash flood and debris flow on the south side of the peak. The Kachina Trail was covered with rocks and mud in two different locations over 300 yards apart. A much smaller flow reached Freidlein Prairie (FR522). Some of the rocks in the flow were 3 to 5 feet in diameter.

Heavy rain over Fremont Peak caused a flash flood and debris flow on the southwest side of the peak. The Kachina Trail and Freidlein Prairie Road were covered with rocks and mud. Some of the rocks in the flow were 3 to 5 feet in diameter.
USGS Gage PLFA3 showed around a 2 ft. rise in the Paria River from 4:15pm to 6:45pm MST.
A landspout was reported near Chino Valley and was on the ground for one to two minutes.
Flooding over Hwy 89 near mile marker 483. Water and debris over highway, closed in both directions for about 30 minutes.
Hail, very heavy rain, and local flooding in area. Quarter sized hail reported.
Stream gauge on Verde River showed rapid 3 foot rise.
Flooding of the Big Chino Wash on Big Springs Ranch Road.
NWS Storm Survey revealed damage was consistent with an EF-1 tornado near the community of Star Valley. The tornado is estimated to have taken place between 09:39 AM and 09:49 AM MST.
Law enforcement reports a possible microburst or tornado. KFSX radar confirms likely tornado. 20 structures damaged, no injuries.
